title: Bootstrap AC emission by hand (no official helper)
when_to_read: When your stack has no official Memex emission helper (you cannot `npm install @memex-ai-ac/vitest`) and need to implement emission natively. The full protocol + behavioural contract to hand-roll a correct emitter in any language.
body:
# Bootstrap AC emission by hand (no official helper)

You're reading this because your codebase has no official Memex emission helper for its language/framework (you can't `npm install @memex-ai-ac/vitest`). This topic is the complete, language-agnostic spec to hand-roll a **correct** emitter — usually ~40–80 lines. Implement it once, wire it into your test runner's "after each test" hook, and your ACs verify exactly like a first-party helper.

## Before you build

- **Prefer an official helper if one exists for your stack.** Check `<server>/docs/examples/` first. Hand-rolling is for stacks we haven't shipped yet.
- **Migrate when an official package ships** for your language. A hand-rolled emitter is yours to maintain; the official one tracks protocol changes for you.
- **Get the behavioural contract right, not just the wire format.** The dangerous mistakes (breaking the build on a failed POST; leaking prod events to localhost) live in the behaviour, not the JSON shape. Section 5 is the part that matters.

## 1. The mental model: the ref IS the routing instruction

Every emission is tagged with an AC's canonical ref: `<namespace>/<memex>/specs/<spec-N>/acs/ac-<N>`. The **namespace prefix decides where the event goes**. You do not hardcode a host; you derive it from the ref. This default routing is the safety mechanism — it stops a `mindset-prod/...` event from being posted to a local or wrong server.

> Never "play it safe" by pointing emission at `http://localhost`. For a `mindset-prod/...` ref that sends the event to the wrong place (or nowhere) and the prod spec silently stays unverified. The default routing IS the safety. (This exact mistake has happened before — don't repeat it.)

## 2. Endpoint + routing table

POST to `<base>/api/test-events`, where `<base>` is derived from the ref's namespace:

| namespace | base URL |
|---|---|
| `mindset-int` | `https://int.memex.ai` |
| `mindset-prod` | `https://memex.ai` |

If the ref's namespace isn't in your table, **default to the SaaS host `https://memex.ai`**: it serves every customer tenant (the namespace selects the workspace, not the host), so a `your-namespace/...` ref still lands. `mindset-int` is the one non-SaaS host that needs an explicit row. Never fall back to localhost. Self-hosters set an explicit `MEMEX_TEST_EVENTS_URL`, which overrides the derived host; for SaaS customers that override is a rare escape hatch, not the normal path.

Method `POST`, `Content-Type: application/json`.

## 3. Authentication

Attach the per-Memex emission key as a Bearer token:

```
Authorization: Bearer <MEMEX_EMIT_KEY>
```

Read the key from the `MEMEX_EMIT_KEY` environment variable. If it's unset, send no Authorization header (the request still goes out; it is rejected once the server enforces keys — see the contract below). To GET a key: call `provision_ac_emission(ref)` (the MCP tool) for a short-lived, spec-scoped key to export as `MEMEX_EMIT_KEY` for this session, or have a human mint a long-lived CI key in Settings → Emission Keys. See `get_information(topic='ac-emission')` for the on-401 flow.

## 4. Payload (JSON body)

```json
{
  "ac_uid": "mindset-prod/your-memex/specs/spec-3/acs/ac-1",
  "status": "pass",
  "test_identifier": "tests/cache_test.py::test_uses_redis",
  "duration_ms": 42,
  "actor": "wic",
  "hidden": false,
  "metadata": { "branch": "main", "commit": "abc123" }
}
```

| field | required | type | notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ac_uid` | yes | string | the full canonical AC ref (the tag) |
| `status` | yes | `"pass" \| "fail" \| "error"` | the test outcome |
| `test_identifier` | yes | string | e.g. `file::name`; groups events per test |
| `duration_ms` | yes | number | test duration in ms |
| `actor` | no | string | WHO ran it; omit if unknown (see fallback chain) |
| `hidden` | no | boolean | true → recorded but excluded from the verification badge |
| `metadata` | no | object<string,string> | context bag; see caps in §5 |

Omit optional fields entirely when you have no value (send no `actor` key rather than `""` or null).

## 5. Behavioural contract (the part that matters)

A correct emitter MUST:

1. **Emit on every finished test — pass, fail, AND error.** The server shows the latest emission per `(ac_uid, test_identifier)`; skipping failures leaves stale green.
2. **Emit once per tagged AC per test.** A test tagged with two ACs emits two events.
3. **NEVER throw / never fail the test run.** Wrap the POST so a non-2xx response OR a network/timeout error is swallowed (log a warning, move on). And bound the request with a client-side timeout (the official helper uses 5s): a HUNG server is as dangerous as a failed one — an unbounded awaited POST stalls past the test framework's hook timeout and fails the test, which swallowing errors alone cannot prevent. Emission is telemetry; it must not be able to break CI. This is non-negotiable — it's what makes it safe to enforce keys server-side without risking red builds.
4. **Respect the off switch.** If `MEMEX_EMIT` is `false` / `0` / `no` / `off` (case-insensitive), make zero HTTP requests. Unset, or any other value, means on.
5. **Respect hidden.** If `MEMEX_HIDDEN` is `true` / `1` / `yes` / `on`, set `hidden: true` on every emission (and/or support a per-tag override).
6. **Stay within metadata caps.** The server enforces ~4KB total / 32 keys / 256 chars per value; over-cap keys are dropped and named in an `X-Memex-Warning` response header (the pass/fail still lands). Keep metadata small, and put no secrets in it — it is visible to anyone who can read the Memex.
7. **Populate actor from the environment** when you can, first match wins: `GITHUB_ACTOR` → `GITLAB_USER_LOGIN` → `BUILDKITE_BUILD_AUTHOR` → `CIRCLE_USERNAME` → `USER` → `USERNAME`. Omit if none are set.
8. **Surface `X-Memex-Warning`** from the response if present (helps the developer fix an over-cap metadata emitter).

## 6. Reference implementation (pseudocode)

```
NAMESPACE_TO_BASE = { "mindset-int": "https://int.memex.ai", "mindset-prod": "https://memex.ai" }

function emit(ac_uid, status, test_identifier, duration_ms, opts):
    off = lower(env("MEMEX_EMIT"))
    if off in {"false", "0", "no", "off"}: return        # off switch

    namespace = ac_uid.split("/")[0]
    base = env("MEMEX_TEST_EVENTS_URL")                  # rare explicit override
           or NAMESPACE_TO_BASE.get(namespace)
    if base is null and namespace != "": base = "https://memex.ai"   # unknown namespace → SaaS default, NEVER localhost
    if base is null: return                              # no namespace at all (malformed ref) → nothing to route
    url = base ends_with "/api/test-events" ? base : base + "/api/test-events"

    payload = { ac_uid, status, test_identifier, duration_ms }
    actor = first_set(GITHUB_ACTOR, GITLAB_USER_LOGIN, BUILDKITE_BUILD_AUTHOR, CIRCLE_USERNAME, USER, USERNAME)
    if actor: payload.actor = actor
    if hidden_enabled(opts): payload.hidden = true
    if opts.metadata: payload.metadata = opts.metadata

    headers = { "Content-Type": "application/json" }
    key = env("MEMEX_EMIT_KEY")
    if key: headers["Authorization"] = "Bearer " + key

    try:
        res = POST(url, headers, json = payload, timeout = 5s)   # bounded — a hang must not stall the suite
        if not res.ok: warn("ac-emit POST " + url + " -> " + res.status)
        if res.header("X-Memex-Warning"): warn(res.header("X-Memex-Warning"))
    catch err:
        warn("ac-emit POST failed: " + err)              # SWALLOW — never rethrow
```

Wire `emit(...)` into your framework's after-each-test hook: derive `status` from the test result and `ac_uid` from however your tests declare their tag (an annotation, a helper call, a naming convention — your choice).

## 7. Port checklist

- [ ] Emits on pass, fail, and error
- [ ] Never throws (non-2xx and network errors both swallowed)
- [ ] Bounds the POST with a client-side timeout (a hung server cannot stall the suite)
- [ ] Derives host from the ref's namespace; defaults unknown namespaces to `https://memex.ai` (never localhost)
- [ ] Sends `Authorization: Bearer $MEMEX_EMIT_KEY` when the key is set
- [ ] Honours `MEMEX_EMIT` off and `MEMEX_HIDDEN`
- [ ] Omits empty optional fields; keeps metadata within caps
- [ ] Populates actor from the env fallback chain

When you're done, run your suite against a spec you can see in Memex and confirm its ACs flip to verified.
